title: Numbers in real life
instruction: Answer these English and maths questions about numbers.

questions.question: Read this: 'The population of Huddersfield is 162,949.' How do you say this number?
options: One hundred and sixty two thousand nine hundred and forty nine
One six two nine four nine
Sixteen thousand two hundred and ninety four
questions.answer: One hundred and sixty two thousand nine hundred and forty nine

questions.question: Which sentence uses an ordinal number correctly?
options: I live on the third floor
questions.answer: I live on the third floor

questions.question: On a form it says 'NI Number'. What does NI stand for?
A bus arrives at 08:45. It is 25 minutes late. What time should it have arrived?
A form asks for your age in months. You are 34 years old. How many months is that?
You need to write your height in cm on a medical form. You are 1.65 metres tall. What do you write?
